text: SimCLR (Chen et al. 2020) demonstrated that a simple framework combining data augmentation, a learnable nonlinear projection head, and contrastive loss can achieve state-of-the-art self-supervised visual representations.
MoCo (He et al. 2020) maintains a dynamic dictionary with a queue and a moving-averaged encoder, enabling contrastive learning with large and consistent negative sample sets.
BYOL (Grill et al. 2020, DeepMind) showed that contrastive learning can work without negative pairs, using a target network updated via exponential moving average to avoid representational collapse.
